corrections.old_value: Useful for engineering increased baicalein/baicalin production in hairy roots or heterologous hosts by introducing the 6-hydroxylation step from chrysin; likely should be stacked with upstream chrysin-forming genes and downstream glycosylation genes for stable baicalin production. Partial silencing causes flux redistribution to chrysin glucuronide, so pathway balance is important.
